candidate_diagnostics.selection_note: active preview source because the existing visual review found useful rainforest texture despite coarse MODIS resolution and shadow/cloud risk

forbidden_use: final water, canopy, wet-rock, or cloud-shadow masks
promotion_blockers: MODIS/GIBS is too coarse for production photoreal materials.
The active April 2 preview has useful rainforest color but still records shadow/cloud risk and must be replaced or supplemented.
No Sentinel/Landsat scene IDs, official orthophoto layers, first-party aerials, or item-level rights-cleared field references are attached yet.
Cloud/shadow metrics are threshold diagnostics only and need manual geospatial review before mask or material authority.
required_next_sources: Copernicus Sentinel scene metadata with acquisition date, processing level, cloud score, CRS, terms, and attribution
Landsat seasonal context scenes for dry/rainfed/high-rain comparison
SNIT/IDECORI official orthophoto layer discovery and terms review if available
first-party or explicit-permission aerial/field references for rainforest canopy, wet rock, and river color
review_findings: March 8, 2025 has the lowest simple cloud-like fraction among retained MODIS/GIBS candidates.
April 2, 2025 remains the active preview seed because it already drives the generated Pacuare drape and has stronger green-dominant rainforest signal, but it also has elevated shadow-like coverage.
All retained candidates are coarse MODIS/GIBS references and are unsuitable for final orthophoto, material, or segmentation authority.
